title: GMP chief addresses two-tier policing concerns after Nowak murder

input.lead: Chief Constable Sir Stephen Watson acknowledges the perception that not everybody is treated equally
input.summary: Chief Constable Sir Stephen Watson acknowledges the perception that not everybody is treated equally
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Police (GMP) Chief Constable Sir Stephen Watson was speaking to BBC North West in the aftermath of the murder of 18-year-old student Henry Nowak and questions over how police treated him in his final moments The murder of Henry Nowak and subsequent police response have prompted accusations of what some have labelled two-tier policing Sir Stephen said he did not believe the perception of two-tier policing was justified in the case of GMP "but I recognise we need to work hard to overturn it" "If you ask for my honest opinion, is there two-tier policing in Manchester, I will say to you absolutely not," said Sir Stephen Two-tier policing is a term used to describe the idea that some groups of people, or some behaviour (for example protests and demonstrations) are dealt with more harshly, more robustly, than others by law enforcement Sir Stephen said GMP "deals with people equally before the law", adding that he believed the operational reality was closer to what people were calling for than they perceived

title: Cheshire and Warrington Combined Authority to sign off spending

input.lead: The combined authority is to sign off its business plan and some initial spending
input.summary: The combined authority is to sign off its business plan and some initial spending
input.evidence_text: Cheshire and Warrington Combined Authority (CWCA) came into being in February and is now set to sign off its business plan at a meeting later Allocating £4.5m from the government-granted Mayoral Investment Fund for projects is set to be approved CWCA has described its first year as "foundational and transitional", ahead of its first mayoral elections in May 2027 An initial budget was set earlier this year and the combined authority is now set to sign off an updated budget as well as its business plan A total of £400,000 is also set to be approved for an economic review, which the combined authority said would "identify choices, trade-offs and constraints" before any political priorities are set by the mayor The authority is set to approve spending £4.5m allocated under the Mayoral Investment Fund

title: Leadership uncertainty costs public money, Simon Case says

input.lead: Speaking on Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg, the former Cabinet Secretary said leadership transitions are "enormously disruptive"
input.summary: Speaking on Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg, the former Cabinet Secretary said leadership transitions are "enormously disruptive"
input.evidence_text: Simon Case, the UK's highest-ranking civil servant from 2020 to 2024, told the BBC's Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg that political upheaval also "costs us money" by driving up borrowing costs His comments come after Andy Burnham won Thursday's Makerfield by-election, paving the way for a Labour leadership challenge Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer is now under pressure from cabinet ministers and a growing number of MPs to set out a plan for his departure and potentially avoid a protracted leadership contest An ally of Sir Keir indicated on Sunday that he was considering what was in the best interest of the country when it came to his political future, while the Tories said a change of leader was unlikely to change how Labour was governing Downing Street said earlier on Sunday that the prime minister was "getting on with the job" and that his position of being "determined to fight for his job" had not changed Burnham's allies have urged Sir Keir to reflect over the weekend and listen to his cabinet ministers, MPs and his family

title: Trespassers on line cause Stockport rail delays

input.lead: Emergency services were called to an incident at about 17:00 BST, National Rail says
input.summary: Emergency services were called to an incident at about 17:00 BST, National Rail says
input.evidence_text: Disruption between Manchester Piccadilly and Wilmslow, Macclesfield and Hazel Grove was expected until 19:45 BST after emergency services were called to the incident at 17:00, National Rail said All lines between these stations had been closed but have since reopened "following trespassers on the railway at Stockport", Avanti West Coast said Passengers are still encouraged to check for updates before travelling due to residual delays A National Rail spokesperson said: "As service recovers, trains may still be cancelled, delayed by up to 60 minutes or diverted. Disruption is expected until 19:45." Listen to the best of BBC Radio Manchester on Sounds and follow BBC Manchester on Facebook , X , and Instagram . You can also send story ideas via Whatsapp to 0808 100 2230

title: New North West bases will 'tackle growing risk of wildfires'

input.lead: Nearly £100m is being invested in teams of specialist firefighters to tackle the growing risk of wildfires
input.summary: Nearly £100m is being invested in teams of specialist firefighters to tackle the growing risk of wildfires
input.evidence_text: Nearly £100m is being invested in teams of specialist firefighters and an overhaul of fire and rescue equipment to tackle the growing risk of wildfires, the government has announced The teams are deployed in strategic locations, which also include Northumberland, London and South Wales, to more quickly ease the pressure on local services as the number of wildfires continues to grow due to hotter and drier conditions brought by climate change Fire services in England tackled nearly 27,000 grassland, woodland and crop fires in the spring and summer last year The teams undertook training over the last few months, increasing their skills, including in specific strategies like tactical burning, and travelled to South Africa and Poland to learn from international counterparts In Lancashire, the existing base at Bacup fire station will take on the new national wildfire role, according to the Local Democracy Reporting Service Chief Fire Officer Jon Charters at Lancashire Fire and Rescue Jon Charters said: "The team has developed into a highly skilled and capable wildfire asset that will now support communities not just locally, but across the UK."

title: Hopes archaeological dig will solve Warrington Castle mystery

input.lead: A project to find Warrington's long-lost medieval castle near to the River Mersey is launched
input.summary: A project to find Warrington's long-lost medieval castle near to the River Mersey is launched
input.evidence_text: They believe they now have enough evidence to prove a castle once stood on the site of St Elphin's Park in the Cheshire town Keith Myers, who is leading the community-led heritage and archaeology project, believes the site near the River Mersey was once a place of strategic and political importance, shaped by Roman, Saxon and Norman activity "We're not saying we have found the castle, but there is now enough evidence to investigate the site carefully, responsibly, and thoroughly," he explained "Historical records, evidence and recent surveys all point to the possibility of significant archaeological remains still present beneath St Elphin's Park," added Myers He said he hoped the work would help to reconnect local people with their heritage "So many historic stories disappear over time, but they still shape the places we live in today

title: Andy Burnham says his Makerfield by-election win could be turning point

input.lead: The outgoing Greater Manchester mayor held off a challenge from Reform UK, behind by more than 9,000 votes
input.summary: The outgoing Greater Manchester mayor held off a challenge from Reform UK, behind by more than 9,000 votes
input.evidence_text: Burnham said his win could mark a "turning point" for the country, while he pledged not to use the constituency as a stepping stone But Prime Minister Sir Keir defended his government's record on delivering change and insisted he would stand in any leadership contest, adding: "I'm not going to walk away." Burnham was joined by several Labour MPs at a victory rally on Friday, while his allies increased their calls for Sir Keir to stand aside and agree a "managed way forward" to avoid a bruising contest There were three by-election results overnight and in Aberdeen South, the Scottish Conservatives won a Westminster by-election for the first time in more than 50 years taking the seat from the SNP; while in Arbroath and Broughty Ferry the SNP held onto the seat in its by-election Burnham won the Makerfield constituency after securing nearly 55% of the vote The former Greater Manchester mayor held off a challenge from Reform UK, which came second but more than 9,000 votes behind Labour

title: Andy Burnham's win means change at the top for Greater Manchester

input.lead: Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor
input.summary: Burnham victory in Makerfield means Greater Manchester must find a new regional mayor
input.evidence_text: Sitting MPs cannot serve as regional mayors, because the regional role incorporates the role of police and crime commissioner The date for the Greater Manchester vote is 30 July Burnham, who won the Makerfield by-election for Labour with a 55% share of the vote, became the region's first directly elected mayor in 2017 , and won two further terms in 2021 and 2024 In 2024, he won 63.4% of the vote, with 420,749 voters backing him He won in every borough in the city region, including in Wigan, where the Makerfield constituency sits The cost of finding a new mayor to replace Burnham is estimated to be about £4.7m - which was one of the reasons Labour gave for blocking his bid to become its candidate in the Gorton and Denton by-election in February

title: Who is Andy Burnham? Returning MP who wants to be prime minister

input.lead: The outgoing Greater Manchester mayor is lining up a third attempt to be Labour leader following his return to Westminster
input.summary: The outgoing Greater Manchester mayor is lining up a third attempt to be Labour leader following his return to Westminster
input.evidence_text: More than 10 years ago he ran twice, unsuccessfully, for the top job Now he's backed by many Labour MPs as the party's best chance of recovery, after months of languishing in the polls and a devastating set of election results in May Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election saw him hold off a challenge from Reform UK, which came second but more than 9,000 votes behind Labour It also saw the former Greater Manchester mayor increase Labour's share of the vote from 45% at the 2024 general election to almost 55% Beyond the numbers, Burnham's victory ensured he cleared a major obstacle to any leadership challenge as those wanting to stand in a contest need to be an MP Before polling day, Burnham said he would seek to enter any potential leadership contest to challenge Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer should he win in Makerfield

title: Sir John Curtice: Burnham's win represents remarkable personal success for him

input.lead: Andy Burnham not only retained the share of the vote Labour won in the seat in 2024 but increased it
input.summary: Andy Burnham not only retained the share of the vote Labour won in the seat in 2024 but increased it
input.evidence_text: Last year in the Runcorn by-election, Labour's vote fell by 14 points. Four months ago in Gorton & Denton, it collapsed by 25 points In Makerfield itself, Labour were 20 points behind Reform in the local elections on 7 May Even at the best of times, support for the party of government nearly always falls in by-elections. Yet, in yesterday's ballot, Burnham not only retained every bit of the 45% share of the vote Labour won in the seat in 2024, but actually pushed his party's share up by 10 points With Labour stuck at just 19% in the national polls, much as it has been ever since last autumn, there has been no evidence of any marked change in Labour's popularity in the last few weeks to account for this turnaround Indeed, Labour saw its vote fall heavily in both the Scottish by-elections also held yesterday – by 19 points in Aberdeen South and 18 points in Arbroath and Broughty Ferry Moreover, polling conducted during the Makerfield campaign suggested Labour would have lost quite badly if anyone other than Burnham had been Labour's standard bearer

title: Historical stone monuments on Winter Hill moors to be rebuilt

input.lead: The Two Lads landmarks are to be rebuilt to make them safer and deter further vandalism
input.summary: The Two Lads landmarks are to be rebuilt to make them safer and deter further vandalism
input.evidence_text: The cairns, on Crooked Edge Hill, an outlying summit on Winter Hill between Lancashire and Greater Manchester, have been a beloved destination of walkers and ramblers for centuries While they were first recorded on maps in 1787, according to Woodland Trust which owns the land, they may also have been mentioned in a land deed from Cecily, widow of Roger de Worsley, in 1280 The trust said after dismantling the cairns there will be a community archaeological dig in August, then they will be rebuilt with the help of a dry stone waller and local volunteers Currently the landmark is made up of one large cairn, another demolished cairn and a third very small construction of stones set some distance away The site is acknowledged by Historic England research records as a monument described as 'Two Lads', 'a confused heap of stones, seems to be the wreckage of one or more cairns' The Woodland Trust, which has owned the land since 2015, has made the decision to rebuild the 'lads' to make them safer

title: Sunday opening hours return to Manchester libraries after 15 years

input.lead: Eight libraries will be open on Sundays for the first time since 2011 when budgets were cut
input.summary: Eight libraries will be open on Sundays for the first time since 2011 when budgets were cut
input.evidence_text: Eight venues will eventually be open on Sundays, with some starting in September and four more following in January 2027 Nine more libraries will also benefit from extended opening hours thanks to an improved council budget which sees £1.7m extra cash for libraries Councillor Shazia Butt said: "We know what a huge miss they have been, and we know the impact that it had all those years ago when we were forced to cut hours." Sunday opening was stopped in 2011 due to budget cuts across the UK, with Manchester one of the hardest hit areas at the time The following will be open on Sundays from September each week from 1200 - 1600 The following will be open on Sundays from January each week from 1200- 1600

title: Greens name councillor as Greater Manchester mayor candidate

input.lead: The Green Party announces Trafford councillor Geraldine Coggins as its candidate to replace Andy Burnham
input.summary: The Green Party announces Trafford councillor Geraldine Coggins as its candidate to replace Andy Burnham
input.evidence_text: Labour is pitching the contest as a two-horse race between Labour and Reform UK, but the Greens are looking to build on their success in the Gorton and Denton by-election with a strong challenge of their own The Greens have named Trafford Council councillor Geraldine Coggins as their candidate Coggins told supporters at a launch event in Manchester it was a straight contest between the Greens and Nigel Farage's Reform UK party as she pledged to improve transport, housing and choose "people and planet over profits" The by-election is scheduled to take place on 30 July, with Labour set to announce its candidate on Friday It was triggered after Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Coggins, a councillor for Trafford since 2018 who is originally from Ireland, is a published academic with a background in leadership, public finance and strategic decision-making, the party said

title: Inmates given whole-life orders for Kyle Bevan murder in HMP Wakefield

input.lead: Mark Fellows, David Taylor and Lee Newell, who stabbed Kyle Bevan in his cell, will never be released
input.summary: Mark Fellows, David Taylor and Lee Newell, who stabbed Kyle Bevan in his cell, will never be released
input.evidence_text: Mark Fellows, 45, David Taylor, 64, and Lee Newell, 57, were found guilty of killing Kyle Bevan at HMP Wakefield on 4 November, using makeshift weapons including one made from a metal part from the back of a television Fellows and Newell were already serving whole-life orders for previous convictions, with judge Mrs Justice McGowan imposing "new and separate" life terms for them at Leeds Crown Court Taylor was given a whole-life order for Bevan's murder, on top of the offences he was on remand for at the time Bevan, 33, was serving a life sentence with a minimum tariff of 28 years for murdering his partner's two-year-old daughter Lola James in Pembrokeshire in 2020 The court heard how Fellows, Taylor and Newell were seen on CCTV at about 17:30 following Bevan into his cell and "cornered him" They emerged less than five minutes later in "a satisfied, job-done mood"

It's silly. It's funny. It's enjoyable "We are very excited to be in Manchester and to hear the crowds go wild." On her unique role, Briony said: "People have said 'Why do we need to have someone dangling off the back of the elephant? We could have just stuck a piece of plastic off the back'

title: Historical stone monuments on Winter Hill moors to be rebuilt

input.lead: A pair of moorland stone landmarks known locally as the 'Two Lads' are to be taken down and rebuilt this summer to make them safer and deter further vandalism The cairns, on Crooked Edge Hill, an outlying summit on Winter Hill between Lancashire and Greater Manchester, have been
input.summary: A pair of moorland stone landmarks known locally as the 'Two Lads' are to be taken down and rebuilt this summer to make them safer and deter further vandalism The cairns, on Crooked Edge Hill, an outlying summit on Winter Hill between Lancashire and Greater Manchester, have been
input.evidence_text: A pair of moorland stone landmarks known locally as the 'Two Lads' are to be taken down and rebuilt this summer to make them safer and deter further vandalism The cairns, on Crooked Edge Hill, an outlying summit on Winter Hill between Lancashire and Greater Manchester, have been a beloved destination of walkers and ramblers for centuries While they were first recorded on maps in 1787, according to Woodland Trust which owns the land, they may also have been mentioned in a land deed from Cecily, widow of Roger de Worsley, in 1280 The trust said after dismantling the cairns there will be a community archaeological dig in August, then they will be rebuilt with the help of a dry stone waller and local volunteers Currently the landmark is made up of one large cairn, another demolished cairn and a third very small construction of stones set some distance away The site is acknowledged by Historic England research records as a monument described as 'Two Lads', 'a confused heap of stones, seems to be the wreckage of one or more cairns'

title: When is it too hot to work under UK law as temperatures forecast to hit 37C

input.lead: Manchester weather When is it too hot to work under UK law as temperatures forecast to hit 37C UK temperatures are set to soar in a heatwave Comments News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 06:15, 22 Jun 2026 View Image Greater Manchester is set for an ex With temperatures expected t
input.summary: Manchester weather When is it too hot to work under UK law as temperatures forecast to hit 37C UK temperatures are set to soar in a heatwave Comments News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 06:15, 22 Jun 2026 View Image Greater Manchester is set for an ex With temperatures expected t
input.evidence_text: Manchester weather When is it too hot to work under UK law as temperatures forecast to hit 37C UK temperatures are set to soar in a heatwave Comments News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 06:15, 22 Jun 2026 View Image Greater Manchester is set for an ex With temperatures expected to hit the mid 30Cs later this week, many of us will be thinking, 'surely we don't have to work in this heat?!' A heatwave is expected to hit the country this week , with Manchester currently forecasted to reach a high of 37C on Thursday. It will no doubt make for uncomfortable and sticky working conditions for workers Unlike some other countries, there is no specific legal limit for workplace temperatures - meaning in the UK there isn't a legally mandated maximum temperature at which employees are automatically sent home from work However, employers are legally obligated to maintain a reasonable and comfortable temperature in the workplace. This means if temperatures become excessively high, creating unsafe or unhealthy conditions, employees may have grounds to raise concerns and potentially leave if they Guidance on the gov.uk website outlines that employers should assess the risks of high temperatures in the workplace and implement control measures, such as providing ventilation, cooling equipment, or allowing for breaks

Juries in two separate trials could not reach verdicts on assault charges siblings Mohammed Fahir Amaaz, 21, and Muhammad Amaad, 26, faced in relation to PC Za A judge formally entered not guilty verdicts in May. title: 'I knew the Preston Davey trial would be truly tragic, but nothing prepared me for what I heard in court'

input.lead: Courts 'I knew the Preston Davey trial would be truly tragic, but nothing prepared me for what I heard in court' 'We were being told to think the unthinkable', court reporter Rachel Smith writes.
input.summary: Courts 'I knew the Preston Davey trial would be truly tragic, but nothing prepared me for what I heard in court' 'We were being told to think the unthinkable', court reporter Rachel Smith writes. The Manchester Evening News and LancsLive covered every In early April, Lancashire P
input.evidence_text: Courts 'I knew the Preston Davey trial would be truly tragic, but nothing prepared me for what I heard in court' 'We were being told to think the unthinkable', court reporter Rachel Smith writes. The Manchester Evening News and LancsLive covered every In early April, Lancashire Police called a press briefing about an upcoming trial. The suspected murder of 13-month-old Preston Davey had been under investigation for almost two years Two men would soon be in the dock at Preston Crown Court. Jamie Varley, 37, and John McGowan-Fazakerley 32, were in the process of adopting Preston when he collapsed at their home in Blackpool They took him into Blackpool Victoria Hospital, where he was pronounced dead at 7.18pm on July 27, 2023. Almost two years later, Varley had been charged with murder, and McGowan-Fazakerley with allowing the death of a child, along with a string of other offences A trial was set to take place from April 20, 2026. Journalists had been invited along to learn more about what to expect from the upcoming proceedings. Nobody was prepared for the horrific details they were about to be told The death of a child is always a tragedy, but the fact a caregiver had been charged with their murder seemed the ultimate betrayal

input.evidence_text: Manchester weather When is it too hot to work under UK law as temperatures forecast to hit 37C UK temperatures are set to soar in a heatwave Comments News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 06:15, 22 Jun 2026 View Image Greater Manchester is set for an ex With temperatures expected to hit the mid 30Cs later this week, many of us will be thinking, 'surely we don't have to work in this heat?!' A heatwave is expected to hit the country this week , with Manchester currently forecasted to reach a high of 37C on Thursday. It will no doubt make for uncomfortable and sticky working conditions for workers Unlike some other countries, there is no specific legal limit for workplace temperatures - meaning in the UK there isn't a legally mandated maximum temperature at which employees are automatically sent home from work However, employers are legally obligated to maintain a reasonable and comfortable temperature in the workplace. This means if temperatures become excessively high, creating unsafe or unhealthy conditions, employees may have grounds to raise concerns and potentially leave if they Guidance on the gov.uk website outlines that employers should assess the risks of high temperatures in the workplace and implement control measures, such as providing ventilation, cooling equipment, or allowing for breaks

Pai, nicknamed The Duck because he walked with a limp due to a long-standing hip defect, was in fact Britain's most powerful Triad godfather From an office above a restaurant in Manchester's Chinatown , the Hong Kong-raised gangster ran an ruthless operation that spread terror among the UK's Chinese communities. And he did so despite repeatedly being kicked out of the country by the authorities One night in the late 70s a Chinese man was having a good run at a casino in Southampton. After winning more than £100, one of his fellow countrymen is said to have sidled to him "Along came Georgie and said 'Lend me £50'," prosecutor Roger Titheridge told Winchester Crown Court in June 1978 as Pai faced extortion charges. input.summary: Courts 'I knew the Preston Davey trial would be truly tragic, but nothing prepared me for what I heard in court' 'We were being told to think the unthinkable', court reporter Rachel Smith writes. The Manchester Evening News and LancsLive covered every In early April, Lancashire P
input.evidence_text: Courts 'I knew the Preston Davey trial would be truly tragic, but nothing prepared me for what I heard in court' 'We were being told to think the unthinkable', court reporter Rachel Smith writes. The Manchester Evening News and LancsLive covered every In early April, Lancashire Police called a press briefing about an upcoming trial. The suspected murder of 13-month-old Preston Davey had been under investigation for almost two years Two men would soon be in the dock at Preston Crown Court. Jamie Varley, 37, and John McGowan-Fazakerley 32, were in the process of adopting Preston when he collapsed at their home in Blackpool They took him into Blackpool Victoria Hospital, where he was pronounced dead at 7.18pm on July 27, 2023. Almost two years later, Varley had been charged with murder, and McGowan-Fazakerley with allowing the death of a child, along with a string of other offences A trial was set to take place from April 20, 2026. Journalists had been invited along to learn more about what to expect from the upcoming proceedings. Nobody was prepared for the horrific details they were about to be told The death of a child is always a tragedy, but the fact a caregiver had been charged with their murder seemed the ultimate betrayal

title: When is it too hot to work under UK law as temperatures forecast to hit 37C

input.lead: Manchester weather When is it too hot to work under UK law as temperatures forecast to hit 37C UK temperatures are set to soar in a heatwave Comments News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 06:15, 22 Jun 2026 View Image Greater Manchester is set for an ex With temperatures expected t
input.summary: Manchester weather When is it too hot to work under UK law as temperatures forecast to hit 37C UK temperatures are set to soar in a heatwave Comments News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 06:15, 22 Jun 2026 View Image Greater Manchester is set for an ex With temperatures expected t
input.evidence_text: Manchester weather When is it too hot to work under UK law as temperatures forecast to hit 37C UK temperatures are set to soar in a heatwave Comments News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 06:15, 22 Jun 2026 View Image Greater Manchester is set for an ex With temperatures expected to hit the mid 30Cs later this week, many of us will be thinking, 'surely we don't have to work in this heat?!' A heatwave is expected to hit the country this week , with Manchester currently forecasted to reach a high of 37C on Thursday. It will no doubt make for uncomfortable and sticky working conditions for workers Unlike some other countries, there is no specific legal limit for workplace temperatures - meaning in the UK there isn't a legally mandated maximum temperature at which employees are automatically sent home from work However, employers are legally obligated to maintain a reasonable and comfortable temperature in the workplace. This means if temperatures become excessively high, creating unsafe or unhealthy conditions, employees may have grounds to raise concerns and potentially leave if they Guidance on the gov.uk website outlines that employers should assess the risks of high temperatures in the workplace and implement control measures, such as providing ventilation, cooling equipment, or allowing for breaks

input.summary: Courts 'I knew the Preston Davey trial would be truly tragic, but nothing prepared me for what I heard in court' 'We were being told to think the unthinkable', court reporter Rachel Smith writes. The Manchester Evening News and LancsLive covered every In early April, Lancashire P
input.evidence_text: Courts 'I knew the Preston Davey trial would be truly tragic, but nothing prepared me for what I heard in court' 'We were being told to think the unthinkable', court reporter Rachel Smith writes. The Manchester Evening News and LancsLive covered every In early April, Lancashire Police called a press briefing about an upcoming trial. The suspected murder of 13-month-old Preston Davey had been under investigation for almost two years Two men would soon be in the dock at Preston Crown Court. Jamie Varley, 37, and John McGowan-Fazakerley 32, were in the process of adopting Preston when he collapsed at their home in Blackpool They took him into Blackpool Victoria Hospital, where he was pronounced dead at 7.18pm on July 27, 2023. Almost two years later, Varley had been charged with murder, and McGowan-Fazakerley with allowing the death of a child, along with a string of other offences A trial was set to take place from April 20, 2026. Journalists had been invited along to learn more about what to expect from the upcoming proceedings. Nobody was prepared for the horrific details they were about to be told The death of a child is always a tragedy, but the fact a caregiver had been charged with their murder seemed the ultimate betrayal

title: Vimto, Greater Manchester's gift to the world

input.evidence_text: Food and drink Vimto, Greater Manchester's gift to the world From a can with your chippy tea, to the homes of Middle Eastern families at Ramadan, Vimto is a Mancunian icon News Stephen Topping Senior reporter 07:08, 21 Jun 2026 View 6 Images The monum It's a Mancunian icon cherished for generations. But Vimto's popularity stretches well beyond the UK For 118 years, the beloved fruity soft drink has been treasured in the city. Vimtonic - as it was originally known - was created by herbalist John Nichols on Granby Row, in Manchester city centre , back in 1908 Production moved to Chapel Street, in Salford , in 1910, Ayres Road, in Old Trafford , in 1927, and Ledson Road, Wythenshawe , from 1971. And while production has long since left Greater Manchester, the brand has remained synonymous with the region The Vimto sculpture has stood at the site of the drink's birthplace since 1992, while the Vimto Gardens development pays homage to Salford's role in the story. Vimto has also graced the homes of many Greater Mancunian families for more than a century - often as the ideal accompan Yet the drink's army of fans truly reaches across the globe. Vimto first became an international brand in 1919, when the trademark was registered in British Guiana in South America, a British colony until 1966 when the country gained independence as Guyana

input.evidence_text: Courts Beaten, starving and penniless, he told police: 'I can't go back there, he'll kill me' Graham Cox was a shell of his former self View 4 Images Graham Cox was found dead at a flat on Barton Walk, Farnworth (Image: Facebook/M.E.N.) News Amy Walker Court reporter 14:43, 20 Jun 2026 Updated 07:13, 21 Jun 2026 Lying in a hospital bed, Graham Cox told police: "I'm terrified. I was locked up." Earlier, the 37-year-old told staff at a community support hub: "I can't go back there, he'll kill me." Beaten, starving and penniless, he was a shell of his former self. Graham - wearing dirty clothes and with a unkempt beard - had escaped from a flat through a window, having been held there without food for two weeks He saw his chance and took it. Now he was pleading for help. Tragically, less than two weeks later he was found dead under a blanket on the sofa at a former friend's home Ashley Crowder, 36, lied to police, telling them: "I was about to phone the ambulance. I've just gone and checked on him." In reality, he had emptied Graham's bank account, attacked him and held him hostage As Crowder went on trial charged with Graham's murder; assault occasioning actual bodily harm; and four counts of theft, a tragic story of a vulnerable man exploited by an opportunist drug addict intent on scoring emerged

Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

title: New North West bases will 'tackle growing risk of wildfires'

input.evidence_text: Nearly £100m is being invested in teams of specialist firefighters and an overhaul of fire and rescue equipment to tackle the growing risk of wildfires, the government has announced The teams are deployed in strategic locations, which also include Northumberland, London and South Wales, to more quickly ease the pressure on local services as the number of wildfires continues to grow due to hotter and drier conditions brought by climate change Fire services in England tackled nearly 27,000 grassland, woodland and crop fires in the spring and summer last year The teams undertook training over the last few months, increasing their skills, including in specific strategies like tactical burning, and travelled to South Africa and Poland to learn from international counterparts In Lancashire, the existing base at Bacup fire station will take on the new national wildfire role, according to the Local Democracy Reporting Service Chief Fire Officer Jon Charters at Lancashire Fire and Rescue Jon Charters said: "The team has developed into a highly skilled and capable wildfire asset that will now support communities not just locally, but across the UK."

title: The Council wants to bring hundreds of empty homes back into use - this is how you can spot one and report it

input.lead: Since the beginning of the year, a major review has been underway around the city’s draft Strategy for bringing empty homes back into use to help meet demand for affordable homes across the city – while also improving communities by tackling
input.summary: Since the beginning of the year, a major review has been underway around the city’s draft Strategy for bringing empty homes back into use to help meet demand for affordable homes across the city – while also improving communities by tackling
input.evidence_text: Since the beginning of the year, a major review has been underway around the city’s draft Strategy for bringing empty homes back into use to help meet demand for affordable homes across the city – while also improving communities by tackling empty homes that can blight an area Following a major public consultation, the Council is now acting on feedback to accelerate plans to bring as many long-term empty homes back into active use as possible – proposals that saw strong public backing from residents (83% of the respondents to the recent consultation ag Although the number of long-term empty homes in Manchester remains close to an historic low – as the Council has managed to reduce the number more than half since 2013 – every void property is a missed opportunity to provide a family a place to live, reduce homelessness and lesse Feedback from Manchester people has confirmed the impact in the city’s neighbourhoods. 86% of respondents to a recent consultation expressed concern about empty homes (51% very concerned) with the majority also reporting that they had seen empty homes fall into disrepair, become What to do if there is an empty home in your community An empty home is often easily recognisable on a street. The easiest way to report an empty home is to report the issue online or by emailing emptyhomesteam@manchester.gov.uk

title: Repurposed flats will help homeless Manchester families

input.lead: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.summary: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.evidence_text: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families The property in Greenwood Road, Woodhouse Park, has been acquired by the Council from registered housing provider Mosscare St Vincent’s as part of wider plans to boost the city’s stock of publicly-owned, quality temporary accommodation Work is due to start this summer, and be completed in December this year, to refurbish the property as 17 self-contained flats for homeless families from Wythenshawe and south Manchester All flats will be refurbished, including new kitchens and bathrooms in each case. Two new laundry rooms for shared use will also be created, and gardens to the rear will have raised bedding for residents’ use The new accommodation will see families supported by an accommodation team based on site to help them move on as quickly as possible into permanent settled tenancies The Greenwood Road initiative is the latest example of the Council’s drive to increase its temporary accommodation stock across the city to reduce the number of out-of-area placements, helping people who become homeless to stay closer to their previous homes and support networks

title: The Greater Manchester Neurodevelopmental (ND) Early Help Model - Stockport Council

input.lead: You may have heard that Greater Manchester is introducing a new way of deciding which children and young people receive an NHS autism or ADHD assessment, and how quickly they are seen.
input.summary: You may have heard that Greater Manchester is introducing a new way of deciding which children and young people receive an NHS autism or ADHD assessment, and how quickly they are seen. We know this may raise questions or concerns for families In this article, we’re sharing clear
input.evidence_text: You may have heard that Greater Manchester is introducing a new way of deciding which children and young people receive an NHS autism or ADHD assessment, and how quickly they are seen. We know this may raise questions or concerns for families In this article, we’re sharing clear and simple information about what these changes mean in Stockport, and where you can get advice and support. Some local details are still being finalised, and we’ll add updates as soon as they are confirmed. For more information, you can also We understand how hard it can be waiting for an assessment, and how difficult it can be to know where to turn for help in the meantime. That’s why we’ve introduced a new way of getting early support from the moment you first notice signs that your child or young person may be aut We know this is a lot to take in. If you’d like to find out more, ask questions, or talk things through, you’re very welcome to come along to one of our information drop-in sessions The Strategic Lead for Neurodivergence in Stockport will be attending the following drop-in sessions supported by the Child and Adolescent Mental Health Service (CAMHS), Parents and Carers Together Stockport (PACTStockport), and Stockport Parents of ADHD Children in Education (SP In-person session with CAMHS and PACTStockport on Monday 11th May 10.30am-12pm, at Alma Lodge, 149 Buxton Road, Stockport, SK2 6EL. Refreshments will be provided. Please reserve a spot for this session

input.lead: 2 new anthologies of poetry, written by Rochdale residents inspired by its year as Greater Manchester Town of Culture, have been published

input.evidence_text: 2 new anthologies of poetry, written by Rochdale residents inspired by its year as Greater Manchester Town of Culture, have been published The first, titled ‘Weaving Words’, is a 120-page collection and captures the uniqueness of the borough, packed with poems celebrating local people and places. The second is aimed specifically at children, called ‘Poems to change the world.’ Both include over 65 new poems from a t Both anthologies (£10 each) are now available to buy from any of the borough’s 16 libraries, or from Rochdale Pioneers Museum on Toad Lane Over the last year, Rochdale’s Poet Laureate Sammy Weaver led writing workshops with residents and over 650 young people, drawing on Rochdale’s co-operative history as a source of inspiration. Many of the poems created feature in the books Councillor Sue Smith, cabinet member for communities and co-operation at Rochdale Borough Council, said “These 2 limited edition anthologies are such a fascinating souvenir of a moment in time. title: Innovative approach to speech and language support earns ministerial praise - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: A government minister has praised Rochdale borough’s award-winning approach to speech, language and communication support which has seen waiting times for families significantly reduce
input.summary: A government minister has praised Rochdale borough’s award-winning approach to speech, language and communication support which has seen waiting times for families significantly reduce
input.evidence_text: A government minister has praised Rochdale borough’s award-winning approach to speech, language and communication support which has seen waiting times for families significantly reduce This recognition came as part of a visit by the Minister of State for School Standards, Georgia Gould, who learned about the innovative new methods that have been introduced locally to help families, schools and early years settings to address children’s communication needs earli Children referred for speech and language therapy previously waited up to 44 weeks for a specialist appointment due to high levels of need in the borough However, local evidence found that in many cases children could often be supported sooner if families and professionals had access to practical tools and guidance at home or in settings like the classroom In response, Rochdale Borough Council and its NHS partners have introduced a new system of early intervention, which enables families and professionals to access instant speech and language support at the touch of a button This is thanks to things like artifical intelligence (AI) technology, tailored speech and language advice through WhatsApp, a coaching platform called Help at Hand, and an online virtual noticeboard which offers practical tips, resources and activities

input.evidence_text: Trading standards officers at Bolton Council have seized a significant quantity of suspected illegal goods following an enforcement operation at a commercial premises in the borough Officers were called to assist Greater Manchester Police (GMP) on 5 June after concerns were raised during a landlord and tenant dispute. title: Health column: Smoke Free Generation - A rare landmark moment for public health

input.lead: It may have become a little lost amid major political and global events but there was a landmark moment for public health last month, the significance of which should not be overlooked On 29th April the government’s Tobacco and Vapes Bill became law, making it illegal to sell tob
input.summary: It may have become a little lost amid major political and global events but there was a landmark moment for public health last month, the significance of which should not be overlooked On 29th April the government’s Tobacco and Vapes Bill became law, making it illegal to sell tob
input.evidence_text: It may have become a little lost amid major political and global events but there was a landmark moment for public health last month, the significance of which should not be overlooked On 29th April the government’s Tobacco and Vapes Bill became law, making it illegal to sell tobacco to anyone born on, or after, 1st January 2009 - and, in doing so, creating the UK’s first ‘smoke‑free generation’ Make no mistake that this is one of the most significant public health reforms in a generation, and one that sends a clear signal: preventing harm before it begins matters Smoking remains the leading preventable cause of illness and early death in the UK. It significantly increases a person’s risk of cancer, heart disease and stroke, places huge pressure on the NHS, and continues to widen the gap in life expectancy across our communities The new law takes a bold, long‑term approach to breaking that cycle by ensuring today’s children never legally start smoking at all We know that information, advice and individual willpower can only go so far. Real, lasting change happens when policy and environments support people to make healthier choices

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

Matthew Bourne’s award-winning The Car Man is back! Loosely based on Bizet’s ever-popular opera, The Car Man has one of the most thrilling and instantly recognisable scores in New Adventures’ repertoire The familiar 19th Century Spanish cigarette factory becomes a greasy 1950’s garage-diner in the American Mid-West where the dreams and passions of a small-town are shattered by the arrival of a handsome and enigmatic stranger. Fuelled by heat and desire, the inhabitants are drive Matthew Bourne’s vivid storytelling combines with one of the most passionately dramatic scores ever written, with music by Terry Davies featuring Rodion Shchedrin’s Carmen Suite (after Bizet’s Carmen), to create a dance thriller like no other The Car Man will ignite your senses and leave you breathless ★★★★★ "Buckle up for the ride of your life. input.evidence_text: Nathaniel Rateliff is a striking, emotive tenor vocalist and songwriter whose work runs the gamut from simple folk and Americana to roots rock & roll to vintage rhythm & blues. He and his band the Night Sweats are based in Denver, Colorado. After fronting two earlier bands, Ratel Rateliff had begun writing songs that required a band, so he formed the Night Sweats. Their rowdy retro sound was deeply influenced by vintage soul, R&B and gospel traditions, Van Morrison and the Band. A formidable live unit with a reputation for stage-quaking shows, they signed Nathaniel Rateliff & the Night Sweats’ self-titled debut album was issued in August 2015, preceded by appearances at the Newport Folk Festival and The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon. They toured globally in support of the album. While still on the road, they issued A Little S Be sure to catch Nathaniel Ratecliff and The Night Sweats live at Albert Hall on the 14th April 2018!

input.lead: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра?
input.summary: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным
input.evidence_text: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным. После аншлагов в Европе и США спектакль впервые приезжает в Лондон 📍 University College London, Level 1, 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL 🕕 DOORS: 18:00 🕖 SHOW: 19:00 «Скамейка» — знаменитая пьеса, когда-то блистательно сыгранная Олегом Ефремовым и Татьяной Дорониной на сцене МХАТа, а затем покорившая театральные площадки по всему миру Это история о мужчине и женщине. О случайной встрече, которая постепенно превращается в откровенный разговор — честный, смешной, ранимый и очень человеческий. О том моменте, когда между людьми исчезают роли и, наконец, становится невозможно притворяться На сцене — Алексей Панин и Ольга Силина. Тонкая актерская игра, живая энергия и удивительно узнаваемая история, в которой каждый услышит что-то своё

title: Michelin-listed Örme reverses closure plans and unveils new direction for the future

input.lead: Just four months after announcing plans to close, the Urmston restaurant is relaunching with a new sharing plates concept Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Back in February, it looked like the end for Örme The Michelin Guide-listed neighbourhood restaurant in Urmston announced
input.summary: Just four months after announcing plans to close, the Urmston restaurant is relaunching with a new sharing plates concept Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Back in February, it looked like the end for Örme The Michelin Guide-listed neighbourhood restaurant in Urmston announced
input.evidence_text: Just four months after announcing plans to close, the Urmston restaurant is relaunching with a new sharing plates concept Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Back in February, it looked like the end for Örme The Michelin Guide-listed neighbourhood restaurant in Urmston announced plans to sell the business and close its doors, citing the ‘significant economic pressures’ facing the hospitality industry and describing long-term sustainability as ‘unfeasible’ Now, in a surprise turnaround, owners have confirmed it will remain open and relaunch on 1 July with a completely new concept centred around sharing plates, social dining and a more relaxed approach to eating out The restaurant, which opened in 2023 and quickly earned recognition from both the Michelin Guide and Good Food Guide, is moving away from its tasting menu format in favour of a regularly changing menu of snacks, small plates and desserts designed to be shared around the table Dishes set to feature on the opening menu include salt’n’vinegar scampi with curry mayo, smoked Lancashire cheese custard on fried bread with red onion jam, cured Cornish mackerel with roasted onion dressing and English peas, and spring lamb served with sweet and sour mint and BB

title: The Manchester charity feeding thousands with restaurant meals

input.lead: Since they started in 2020, Eat Well MCR is a lifeline to people across the region By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 17 June 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Since it started in the days after lockdown, Eat Well MCR has delivered more than 150,000 meals to people who n
input.summary: Since they started in 2020, Eat Well MCR is a lifeline to people across the region By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 17 June 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Since it started in the days after lockdown, Eat Well MCR has delivered more than 150,000 meals to people who n
input.evidence_text: Since they started in 2020, Eat Well MCR is a lifeline to people across the region By Manchester's Finest, Last updated 17 June 2026 Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Since it started in the days after lockdown, Eat Well MCR has delivered more than 150,000 meals to people who need them across Greater Manchester – all cooked by some of the city’s most prestigious restaurants Recruiting the like of Dishoom , Hawksmoor , Hello Oriental and Maray , among many, many others (too many to name check here), they give back to the city without fanfare and for all the right reasons We spent a day out on the road with some of the team, to see first-hand some of the vital work they do – from the kitchens making up the meals all the way through to the people who receive them The whole thing is magnificent; a testament both to the tenacity of its founders, Kathleen O’Connor, Gemma Saunders and Mary-Ellen McTague, and also to the city’s chefs who quietly but diligently support what has become an essential lifeline for so many

title: Manchester Airport to open first-ever Wetherspoons pub

input.lead: The new Terminal 2 venue will open in September, inspired by one of Manchester’s most famous entertainment destinations Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Manchester Airport has revealed the name and first look at its long-awaited JD Wetherspoon pub, which will open in Terminal
input.summary: The new Terminal 2 venue will open in September, inspired by one of Manchester’s most famous entertainment destinations Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Manchester Airport has revealed the name and first look at its long-awaited JD Wetherspoon pub, which will open in Terminal
input.evidence_text: The new Terminal 2 venue will open in September, inspired by one of Manchester’s most famous entertainment destinations Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Manchester Airport has revealed the name and first look at its long-awaited JD Wetherspoon pub, which will open in Terminal 2 this September The airport’s first-ever ‘Spoons will be called The Belle Vue, a tribute to one of Manchester’s most famous entertainment destinations. For well over a century, Belle Vue was at the heart of the city’s social life, hosting everything from zoological gardens and funfairs to greyho Now, the name is set for a new chapter, this time in the departures lounge Spanning 338 sq m and seating more than 300 people, The Belle Vue has been designed to reflect both the history of its namesake and the North West’s enduring love affair with sport. Newly released images show a clubhouse-inspired interior blending classic pub touches with contemp For many travellers, a pre-flight pint is as much a part of the holiday ritual as checking in, and The Belle Vue looks set to become one of Terminal 2’s busiest gathering spots when it opens later this year

input.evidence_text: One of the things that struck me listening to Alan Milburn's interim review on young people and work was his acknowledgement that employers, particularly SMEs, will need more support if they are expected to play a bigger role in helping young people transition into employment At almost exactly the same time, the government announced further changes to T Level industry placements, relaxing some of the original requirements after years of challenges securing enough employer participation. The move follows a significant decline in placements, with FE Wee To me, those two things are closely connected, for years, the conversation around skills and early careers has focused heavily on supply. More qualifications, more bootcamps, more people trained, more pathways into industry Those things matter. But creating talent is only half the equation. The other half is making sure employers have the capacity, confidence and support to absorb that talent Over the last year, Manchester Digital has been running TConnect, a pilot supported by the Gatsby Foundation and Greater Manchester Combined Authority that aimed to make it easier for employers to engage with T Level placements. Like most pilots, it raised as many questions as it One thing became clear very quickly. The challenge is rarely convincing employers that workplace experience matters, most employers already believe that. The new festival is "Greater Manchester's tech story has changed," said Katie Gallagher, Managing Director of Manchester Digital. "We're no longer making the case that this region matters - that case is made. It was emotionally unguarded in her music, and uncompromising in public – used to speak uncomfortable truths even when doing so would bring personal and professional damage. The Surge: An Ode to Sinéad O’Connor takes Conceived, choreographed and directed by the Tony Award-winning Sonya Tayeh, The Surge is the first major stage production devoted to O’Connor’s life and work, presented by New York’s Joyce Theater and Factory International . Performed by a company of 10 women spanning a wide ran “Sinéad has been a part of my life since her first album arrived blaring out car windows in Detroit, where I’m from,” Sonya Tayeh told Crack Magazine . “All of those memories came flooding back when she passed. I was heartbroken and had this sudden vision of 10 women standing in That initial image grew into a deliberate challenge to how age is typically framed in the dance world. The performers cut through the idea of ageing as something to fear, conceal or overcome. Time is treated as a privilege that carries both experience and beauty

input.summary: Manchester City winger Jeremy Doku has been criticised after an L'Equipe journalist called out his decision to leave Belgium's World Cup squad

L'Equipe journalist France Pierron has sparked considerable controversy with remarks that have been clipped Doku featured in Belgium's 1-1 draw with Egypt in their opening World Cup fixture, but could sit out some of the Red Devils' group stage matches to be with his partner during the birth this month. Speaking on the L'Equipe channel, presenter Pierron said: "You can't miss a World C "You have the chance to participate in a World Cup, and there are hundreds of footballers who would kill to be in your place, but you're going to leave all that to go and witness the birth of your child, which is a disgusting moment, excuse me, where the father is useless, he has However, not everyone on the panal agreed. What do you mean, we're useless?" former boxer Brahim Asloum said. "Who's encouraging us?" Pierron said: "The midwife doesn't know how to do it? Meanwhile, you're going to waste 10 hours, you're going to get exhausted, you're going to have an emotional meltdown, you're going to be completely deflated by your World Cup."
